\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Bonami}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.Hypercontractivity.Bonami
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Overview}

This file develops the notion of $B$-reasonability of a real random variable and proves
the Bonami lemma: a Boolean function of degree at most $k$ is $9^{k}$-reasonable under the
uniform measure on the hypercube. Along the way it establishes tail and anti-concentration
bounds (including the Paley--Zygmund inequality), the average/difference decomposition of a
Boolean function over its last coordinate, and the associated moment and Fourier identities.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Declarations}

\begin{definition}[$B$-reasonable random variable]
\lean{Bonami.IsBReasonable}
\leanok
A real random variable $X$ on a measurable space $\Omega$ with measure $P$ is
\emph{$B$-reasonable} when its fourth moment is controlled by its second moment, namely
\[
\E[X^4] \le B \cdot \bigl(\E[X^2]\bigr)^2 .
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Tail bound for $B$-reasonable variables]
\lean{Bonami.b_reasonable_tail_bound}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.IsBReasonable}
If $X$ is $B$-reasonable and not equivalent to $0$ (its second moment is positive), then for
every $t > 0$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,|X| \ge t\,\norm{X}_2\,\bigr] \le \frac{B}{t^4}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[$B$-reasonability from minimal probability]
\lean{Bonami.min_prob_b_reasonable}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.IsBReasonable}
Let $X$ be a discrete random variable on a finite space whose law is a PMF $\pi$. If
$\mu > 0$ is a lower bound on every atom probability $\pi(\omega)$, then $X$ is
$(1/\mu)$-reasonable.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Paley--Zygmund inequality]
\lean{Bonami.paley_zygmund_ineq}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.degree_zero_fourth_moment}
Let $Z \ge 0$ be a nonnegative random variable with finite first and second moments and
positive mean. For every $\theta$ with $0 \le \theta \le 1$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,Z > \theta\,\E[Z]\,\bigr] \ge \frac{(1-\theta)^2\,(\E[Z])^2}{\E[Z^2]}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Anti-concentration for $B$-reasonable variables]
\lean{Bonami.b_reasonable_anticon_zero}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.IsBReasonable, Bonami.paley_zygmund_ineq}
If $X$ is $B$-reasonable and not equivalent to $0$, then for every $t \in [0,1]$,
\[
\Pr\bigl[\,|X| > t\,\norm{X}_2\,\bigr] \ge \frac{(1-t^2)^2}{B}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Restriction of the last coordinate]
\lean{Bonami.restrictLast}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc}
Given a Boolean function $f$ on $n+1$ variables and a bit $b$, the restriction
$\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,b$ is the function on $n$ variables obtained by fixing the last
coordinate to $b$, i.e.\ $x \mapsto f(x, b)$.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}[Average over the last coordinate]
\lean{Bonami.avgLast}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.restrictLast, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc}
The average $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on $n$
variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} + \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}[Half-difference over the last coordinate]
\lean{Bonami.diffLast}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.restrictLast, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc}
The half-difference $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ of $f$ over its last coordinate is the function on
$n$ variables given by $\tfrac12\bigl(\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false} - \mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\bigr)$.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Restriction at false]
\lean{Bonami.restrictLast_false_eq}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.restrictLast, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc}
For every $x$, the restriction of $f$ fixing the last coordinate to false equals the sum of
the average and the half-difference: $\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{false}\,x = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f\,x + \mathrm{diffLast}\,f\,x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Restriction at true]
\lean{Bonami.restrictLast_true_eq}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.restrictLast, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc}
For every $x$, the restriction of $f$ fixing the last coordinate to true equals the difference
of the average and the half-difference: $\mathrm{restrictLast}\,f\,\mathrm{true}\,x = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f\,x - \mathrm{diffLast}\,f\,x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Sum over the hypercube splits on the last bit]
\lean{Bonami.sum_boolCube_succ}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube}
For any $\varphi$ on the cube of dimension $n+1$, the total sum decomposes according to the
value of the last coordinate:
\[
\sum_{x \in \{0,1\}^{n+1}} \varphi(x) = \sum_{x \in \{0,1\}^{n}} \varphi(x,\mathrm{false}) + \sum_{x \in \{0,1\}^{n}} \varphi(x,\mathrm{true}).
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Uniform weight halves under one extra variable]
\lean{Bonami.uniformWeight_succ}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
The uniform point weight satisfies $\mathrm{uniformWeight}(n+1) = \mathrm{uniformWeight}(n)/2$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Fourier coefficient of the average]
\lean{Bonami.fourierCoeff_avgLast}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.restrictLast, Bonami.sum_boolCube_succ, Bonami.uniformWeight_succ, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.chiS, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct}
For each $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the Fourier coefficient of $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ at $S$
equals the Fourier coefficient of $f$ at the image of $S$ under \texttt{castSucc}:
$\widehat{\mathrm{avgLast}\,f}(S) = \widehat{f}(\mathrm{castSucc}(S))$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Fourier coefficient of the half-difference]
\lean{Bonami.fourierCoeff_diffLast}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.restrictLast, Bonami.uniformWeight_succ, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.chiS, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct}
For each $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, the Fourier coefficient of $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ at $S$
equals the Fourier coefficient of $f$ at the image of $S$ under \texttt{castSucc} together with
the last index: $\widehat{\mathrm{diffLast}\,f}(S) = \widehat{f}\bigl(\mathrm{castSucc}(S) \cup \{\mathrm{last}\,n\}\bigr)$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Expectation splits over the last coordinate]
\lean{Bonami.expect_succ_eq}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.restrictLast, Bonami.sum_boolCube_succ, Bonami.uniformWeight_succ, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect}
The expectation of $\varphi$ on the cube of dimension $n+1$ is the average of the expectations
of its two restrictions:
\[
\E[\varphi] = \tfrac12\bigl(\E[\mathrm{restrictLast}\,\varphi\,\mathrm{false}] + \E[\mathrm{restrictLast}\,\varphi\,\mathrm{true}]\bigr).
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Fourth-power algebraic identity]
\lean{Bonami.fourth_pow_sum}
\leanok
For all real $a, b$, $(a+b)^4 + (a-b)^4 = 2\,(a^4 + 6\,a^2 b^2 + b^4)$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Second-power algebraic identity]
\lean{Bonami.second_pow_sum}
\leanok
For all real $a, b$, $(a+b)^2 + (a-b)^2 = 2\,(a^2 + b^2)$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Fourth-moment decomposition]
\lean{Bonami.fourth_moment_decomp}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.expect_succ_eq, Bonami.restrictLast, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect}
Writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and $h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the fourth moment of $f$
decomposes as
\[
\E[f^4] = \E[g^4] + 6\,\E[g^2 h^2] + \E[h^4].
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Second-moment decomposition]
\lean{Bonami.second_moment_decomp}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.restrictLast, Bonami.restrictLast_true_eq, Bonami.sum_boolCube_succ, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
Writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and $h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the second moment of $f$
decomposes as $\E[f^2] = \E[g^2] + \E[h^2]$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Cauchy--Schwarz for the expectation]
\lean{Bonami.expect_cs_sq}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
For Boolean functions $g, h$ on the cube, $\bigl(\E[g^2 h^2]\bigr)^2 \le \E[g^4]\cdot\E[h^4]$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Nonnegativity of $\E[f^2]$]
\lean{Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect}
For every Boolean function $f$, $0 \le \E[f^2]$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Nonnegativity of $\E[g^2 h^2]$]
\lean{Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg_prod}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect}
For Boolean functions $g, h$, $0 \le \E[g^2 h^2]$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Nonnegativity of $\E[f^4]$]
\lean{Bonami.expect_fourth_nonneg}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg_prod, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect}
For every Boolean function $f$, $0 \le \E[f^4]$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Degree bound for the average]
\lean{Bonami.degree_avgLast}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.restrictLast, Bonami.sum_boolCube_succ, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.chiS, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.has_degree_at_most, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
If $f$ has degree at most $k$, then its average over the last coordinate $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$
also has degree at most $k$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Degree bound for the half-difference]
\lean{Bonami.degree_diffLast}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.restrictLast, Bonami.uniformWeight_succ, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.chiS, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.has_degree_at_most, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct}
If $f$ has degree at most $k$, then its half-difference over the last coordinate
$\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ has degree at most $k-1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Degree-zero functions are constant]
\lean{Bonami.degree_zero_const}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.chiS, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.has_degree_at_most, BooleanAnalysis.walsh_expansion}
A Boolean function $f$ of degree at most $0$ is constant: $f(x) = f(\mathrm{default})$ for all
$x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Fourth moment of a constant function]
\lean{Bonami.degree_zero_fourth_moment}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.degree_zero_const, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.has_degree_at_most, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
For a Boolean function $f$ of degree at most $0$ (hence constant),
$\E[f^4] = \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^2$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Algebraic inductive step for Bonami]
\lean{Bonami.bonami_algebra}
\leanok
The key algebraic inequality driving the inductive step of the Bonami lemma: for
$a, b, A, B, C \ge 0$ (with $a, b, B, C$ nonnegative) satisfying
$A \le 9^{m+1} a^2$, $B \le 9^m b^2$, and $C^2 \le A\cdot B$, one has
\[
A + 6C + B \le 9^{m+1}\,(a+b)^2.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Bonami inequality in expectation form]
\lean{Bonami.bonami_expect}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.bonami_algebra, Bonami.degree_avgLast, Bonami.degree_diffLast, Bonami.degree_zero_fourth_moment, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.expect_cs_sq, Bonami.expect_fourth_nonneg, Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg, Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg_prod, Bonami.fourth_moment_decomp, Bonami.second_moment_decomp, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.has_degree_at_most, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
The main Bonami inequality stated through the expectation operator: if a Boolean function $f$
has degree at most $k$, then
\[
\E[f^4] \le 9^{k}\,\bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^2.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Moments equal expectations under the uniform law]
\lean{Bonami.moment_eq_expect}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
If $P$ is a probability measure on the cube assigning each point the uniform weight, then the
$p$-th moment of $f$ under $P$ coincides with the expectation $\E[f^p]$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Uniform measure on the hypercube]
\lean{Bonami.uniformMeasure}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube}
The canonical uniform probability measure on the Boolean hypercube $\{0,1\}^n$.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Uniform measure of a point]
\lean{Bonami.uniformMeasure_apply}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.uniformMeasure, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
The uniform measure assigns each point $x$ of the cube the combinatorial uniform weight:
$(\mathrm{uniformMeasure}\,n)(\{x\}) = \mathrm{uniformWeight}\,n$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Bonami lemma]
\lean{Bonami.bonami_lemma}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.IsBReasonable, Bonami.bonami_expect, Bonami.moment_eq_expect, Bonami.uniformMeasure, Bonami.uniformMeasure_apply, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.has_degree_at_most}
A Boolean function $f$ of degree at most $k$ is $9^{k}$-reasonable under the uniform measure on
the hypercube.
\end{lemma}
